Let $f : \mathbb{R}\_+ \to \mathbb{R}\_+$ be a function from the set of positive
real numbers to the same set satisfying $f(f(x)) = x$ for all positive $x$.
Suppose that $f$ is infinitely differentiable for all positive $x$, and that
$f(a) \ne a$ for some positive $a$. Prove that $\displaystyle{\lim_{x \to \infty} f(x) = 0}$.
